title: WorkCorrelation v1
description: Thin cross-project correlation record. Maestro mints work_item_id (its own task/workstream id, inherited by consumers verbatim); status is a lossy surjective projection of the source vocabulary onto a minimal common enum, with source_status kept verbatim for auditability. See rationale.md. evidence_refs[] carries typed evidence pointers (EvidenceRef v1, roadmap phase 3).

work_item_id.description: Cross-project join key. Minted by Maestro (= task.id / workstream.id). Derived deterministically for children: '<parent_work_item_id>/<source_local_id>'.

parent_work_item_id.description: Hierarchy link (e.g. spec-runner task -> owning Maestro workstream).

status.description: Minimal common enum. Universal exits: failed, cancelled. Recovery: pending. Surjective projection only — never a claim of semantic equivalence.

source_project.description: Project owning the local record (e.g. maestro, spec-runner, arbiter).

source_local_id.description: Local id verbatim (e.g. Maestro task.id, spec-runner TASK-nnn).

source_status.description: Local status VERBATIM. Without it the projection loses auditability.

source_locator.description: Disambiguates local ids that are not globally unique (e.g. spec_dir for spec-runner TASK-nnn).

pipeline_id.description: Run context: Maestro session ULID (= logs/<ULID>/ directory name).

trace_id.description: W3C trace id from the observability contract.

ts.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the source record.

evidence_refs.description: Typed pointers to evidence about this work item (added in roadmap phase 3, additive pre-adoption). Canonical shape: contracts/observability/evidence-ref.schema.json; the inline definition below is kept structurally identical (parsed-JSON equality, top-level metadata excluded) by a sync test.

kind.description: What the pointer refers to; drives which keys are required (see evidence-ref.md).

pipeline_id.description: Maestro session ULID = logs/<ULID>/ directory (kind=log: required).

path.description: Project-relative path (kind=artifact: required). Absolute paths and `..` segments are rejected by the pattern — dereferencers must never be steered outside the owning repo.

gate_id.description: Gate identifier the verdict was recorded for (kind=gate-verdict: required).

sha.description: Full commit SHA the verdict is bound to (kind=gate-verdict: required; WS-006 DESIGN-608 — a new commit invalidates verdicts).

note.description: Optional free-text hint for humans; never machine-parsed.
